content.full_text:
Assembly
California Legislature

DORIS ALLEN
ASSEMBLYWOMAN, SEVENTY-FIRST DISTRICT
January 25, 1991

Mark Morodoni, Esq.
Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Dear Mark:

I am writing to respectfully request written confirmation of information provided by you to Sam Roth of my staff concerning any and all Proposition 73 limitations and transfer bans remaining in effect for the upcoming legislative special elections set for March 19, 1991.

I would appreciate receiving written confirmation and any other information you may wish to provide by the close of business on Friday, February 1, due to the proximity of the special election.

As I understand the information you provided; the FPPC is enforcing individual ($1000) and political action committee ($5000) limitations for special elections for legislative seats, including those scheduled for March 19, 1991.

In addition, the FPPC is enforcing a ban on any "inter" candidate transfer of funds in such elections, as long as the receiving committee is controlled by a candidate in such elections.

However, "intra" candidate transfers of funds from other committees controlled by the candidate running in the special election are permitted, subject to the same contribution limitations.

Also, records must be kept for such "intra" candidate transfers which detail the source of the transferred funds (donations). You also indicated that any method (i.e., last in first out) for transferring the funds was acceptable, provided records are kept.

January 25, 1991

Again, I would appreciate your written confirmation of this information by February 1, 1991. Please feel free to forward any and all relevant FPPC advisories, guidelines or opinions issued to date governing special elections for legislative seats.

Thank you for your prompt attention to this request and for your continued cooperation and support.

Sincerely,

[Signature]
DORIS ALLEN
Assemblywoman
